subject: Python 3.3 support
truth: Stop using Python 3.3; Tornado 5.0 requires Python 3.4+.
quote: Python 3.3 is no longer supported.

subject: Python 2.7 versions before 2.7.9
truth: Stop using Python 2.7 versions older than 2.7.9; Tornado 5.0 requires ssl.SSLContext, ssl.create_default_context, and ssl.match_hostname.
quote: Versions of Python 2.7 that predate the `ssl` module update are no

subject: Python 3.5 versions before 3.5.2
truth: Stop using Python 3.5 versions older than 3.5.2; Tornado 5.0 requires 3.5.2+ due to async iterator protocol changes.
quote: Versions of Python 3.5 prior to 3.5.2 are no longer supported due to a change in the async iterator protocol in that version.

subject: trollius project support
truth: Stop using the trollius project; Tornado 5.0 no longer supports asyncio backported to Python 2 via trollius.
quote: The trollius project (asyncio backported to Python 2) is no longer supported.

subject: Future.exc_info and Future.set_exc_info methods
truth: Stop using Future.exc_info() and Future.set_exc_info(); use tornado.concurrent.future_set_exc_info to set exceptions and raise the exception with Future.result() to retrieve it.
quote: `tornado.concurrent.Future` is now an alias for `asyncio.Future`

subject: io_loop arguments to many Tornado functions
old: io_loop parameter passed explicitly to functions
truth: Stop passing io_loop arguments to Tornado functions; use IOLoop.current() instead of explicitly passing IOLoop objects.
quote: io_loop arguments to many Tornado functions have been removed. Use .IOLoop.current() instead of passing .IOLoop objects explicitly.

from_fact: io_loop parameter passed explicitly to functions

subject: IOLoop.configure on Python 3
old: IOLoop.configure could select different IOLoop implementations
new: IOLoop.configure is effectively removed on Python 3 (may only redundantly specify the asyncio-backed IOLoop)
truth: Stop relying on IOLoop.configure to switch IOLoop implementations on Python 3; it is effectively removed and only redundantly specifies the asyncio-backed IOLoop.
quote: On Python 3, .IOLoop is always a wrapper around the asyncio event loop. IOLoop.configure is effectively removed on Python 3 (for compatibility, it may be called to redundantly specify the asyncio-backed .IOLoop)

from_fact: ioloop.configure could select different ioloop implementations
ioloop.configure is effectively removed on python 3 (may only redundantly specify the asyncio-backed ioloop)

subject: IOLoop.instance
new: IOLoop.current()
truth: Stop using IOLoop.instance(); use IOLoop.current() instead, and use your own global variable for cross-thread communication.
quote: .IOLoop.instance is now a deprecated alias for .IOLoop.current. Applications that need the cross-thread communication behavior facilitated by .IOLoop.instance should use their own global variable instead.

subject: tornado.concurrent.Future on Python 3
new: alias for asyncio.Future
truth: On Python 3, tornado.concurrent.Future is now an alias for asyncio.Future, not Tornado's own Future class.
quote: `tornado.concurrent.Future` is now an alias for `asyncio.Future` when running on Python 3.

subject: tornado.gen coroutine runner on Python 3
new: asyncio.Task
truth: On Python 3, asyncio.Task is used instead of the Tornado coroutine runner for coroutines.
quote: On Python 3, `asyncio.Task` is used instead of the Tornado coroutine runner.

subject: HTTPServer malformed request handling
old: server simply closes connection on malformed request
new: server responds with 400 error
truth: If a client sends a malformed request, the server now responds with a 400 error instead of simply closing the connection.
quote: If a client sends a malformed request, the server now responds with a 400 error instead of simply closing the connection.

from_fact: server simply closes connection on malformed request

subject: HTTPServerRequest string representation
new: string representation no longer includes request headers
truth: Stop relying on .HTTPServerRequest string representation containing request headers; it no longer includes them.
quote: The string representation of `.HTTPServerRequest` objects (which are sometimes used in log messages) no longer includes the request headers.

from_fact: string representation no longer includes request headers

subject: IOLoop.initialized
truth: Stop calling IOLoop.initialized(); it has been removed.
quote: The IOLoop.initialized method has been removed.

subject: IOLoop implementation on Python 3
old: alternative IOLoop implementations could be configured on Python 3
new: asyncio-backed IOLoop is always used on Python 3; alternatives cannot be configured
truth: On Python 3, stop trying to configure alternative IOLoop implementations; only the asyncio-backed IOLoop is available.
quote: On Python 3, the asyncio-backed .IOLoop is always used and alternative .IOLoop implementations cannot be configured.

from_fact: alternative ioloop implementations could be configured on python 3
asyncio-backed ioloop is always used on python 3; alternatives cannot be configured

subject: io_loop argument to PeriodicCallback
truth: Stop passing the io_loop argument to PeriodicCallback; it has been removed.
quote: The io_loop argument to .PeriodicCallback has been removed.

subject: io_loop argument to IOStream constructor
truth: Stop passing the io_loop argument to IOStream(); it has been removed.
quote: The io_loop argument to the .IOStream constructor has been removed.

subject: io_loop arguments in netutil
old: io_loop arguments to add_accept_handler, ExecutorResolver, ThreadedResolver
truth: Stop passing io_loop arguments to add_accept_handler, ExecutorResolver, and ThreadedResolver; they have been removed.
quote: The io_loop arguments to .add_accept_handler, .ExecutorResolver, and .ThreadedResolver have been removed.

from_fact: io_loop arguments to add_accept_handler, executorresolver, threadedresolver

subject: IOLoop.install and IOLoop.clear_instance
truth: Stop using IOLoop.install() and IOLoop.clear_instance(); they are deprecated.
quote: .IOLoop.install and .IOLoop.clear_instance are deprecated.

subject: ExecutorResolver, BlockingResolver, ThreadedResolver
new: default Resolver using IOLoop.run_in_executor
truth: Stop using ExecutorResolver, BlockingResolver, and ThreadedResolver; they are deprecated in favor of the default Resolver.
quote: ExecutorResolver, .BlockingResolver, and .ThreadedResolver are deprecated.

subject: AsyncIOLoop and AsyncIOMainLoop
new: they are used automatically; explicit reference is no longer recommended
truth: Stop explicitly referencing AsyncIOLoop and AsyncIOMainLoop; they are now used automatically.
quote: `.AsyncIOLoop` and `.AsyncIOMainLoop` are now used automatically when appropriate; referencing them explicitly is no longer recommended.

from_fact: they are used automatically; explicit reference is no longer recommended

subject: IOLoop starting/making current
old: starting an IOLoop or making it current did not set the asyncio event loop
new: starting an IOLoop or making it current now also sets the asyncio event loop for the current thread
truth: Starting an IOLoop or making it current now also sets the asyncio event loop for the current thread; closing an IOLoop closes the corresponding asyncio event loop.
quote: Starting an `.IOLoop` or making it current now also sets the `asyncio` event loop for the current thread. Closing an `.IOLoop` closes the corresponding `asyncio` event loop.

from_fact: starting an ioloop or making it current did not set the asyncio event loop
starting an ioloop or making it current now also sets the asyncio event loop for the current thread

subject: to_tornado_future and to_asyncio_future
new: they are now no-ops and deprecated
truth: Stop calling to_tornado_future and to_asyncio_future; they are deprecated no-ops.
quote: `.to_tornado_future` and `.to_asyncio_future` are deprecated since they are now no-ops.

from_fact: they are now no-ops and deprecated

subject: TLS configuration in SimpleAsyncHTTPClient
old: TLS was configured differently (not via ssl.create_default_context)
new: TLS is now configured according to ssl.create_default_context by default
truth: TLS in SimpleAsyncHTTPClient now uses ssl.create_default_context by default, which may change certificate validation behavior.
quote: TLS is now configured according to `ssl.create_default_context` by default.

from_fact: tls was configured differently (not via ssl.create_default_context)
tls is now configured according to ssl.create_default_context by default

subject: RequestHandler.set_status
old: set_status required the status code to be in http.client.responses
new: set_status no longer requires the status code to appear in http.client.responses
truth: set_status no longer requires the status code to be in http.client.responses; previously invalid status codes now work.
quote: `.RequestHandler.set_status` no longer requires that the given status code appear in `http.client.responses`.

from_fact: set_status required the status code to be in http.client.responses
set_status no longer requires the status code to appear in http.client.responses

subject: sending body with 1xx or 204 responses
old: it was allowed to send a body with 1xx or 204 responses
new: it is no longer allowed to send a body with 1xx or 204 responses
truth: Stop sending a body with 1xx or 204 responses; it is no longer allowed.
quote: It is no longer allowed to send a body with 1xx or 204 responses.

from_fact: it was allowed to send a body with 1xx or 204 responses
it is no longer allowed to send a body with 1xx or 204 responses

subject: RedirectHandler query arguments
old: RedirectHandler did not copy query arguments from the request to the redirect location
new: RedirectHandler now copies any query arguments from the request to the redirect location
truth: RedirectHandler now copies query arguments from the request to the redirect location; existing code relying on them being dropped will break.
quote: `.RedirectHandler` now copies any query arguments from the request to the redirect location.

from_fact: redirecthandler did not copy query arguments from the request to the redirect location
redirecthandler now copies any query arguments from the request to the redirect location

subject: WebSocket invalid headers response
old: requests with invalid websocket headers got a closed connection
new: requests with invalid websocket headers now get a 400 status code response
truth: Requests with invalid websocket headers now get a 400 response instead of a closed connection.
quote: Requests with invalid websocket headers now get a response with status code 400 instead of a closed connection.

from_fact: requests with invalid websocket headers got a closed connection
requests with invalid websocket headers now get a 400 status code response

subject: WebSocketHandler.write_message raises WebSocketClosedError
old: write_message did not raise WebSocketClosedError if connection closed during write
new: write_message now raises WebSocketClosedError if the connection closes while the write is in progress
truth: WebSocketHandler.write_message now raises WebSocketClosedError if the connection closes during the write; catch this exception.
quote: `.WebSocketHandler.write_message` now raises `.WebSocketClosedError` if the connection closes while the write is in progress.

from_fact: write_message did not raise websocketclosederror if connection closed during write
write_message now raises websocketclosederror if the connection closes while the write is in progress

subject: routing default 404
old: no default 404 response was generated if no delegate was found
new: a default 404 response is now generated if no delegate is found for a request
truth: A default 404 response is now generated if no delegate is found for a request; previously this might have been silent or different.
quote: A default 404 response is now generated if no delegate is found for a request.

from_fact: no default 404 response was generated if no delegate was found
a default 404 response is now generated if no delegate is found for a request

subject: Python 2.7 and 3.4 support
truth: Stop using Python 2.7 or 3.4; the minimum supported Python version is now 3.5.2.
quote: Python 2.7 and 3.4 are no longer supported; the minimum supported Python version is 3.5.2.

subject: tornado.stack_context module
truth: Stop importing tornado.stack_context; the module has been removed.
quote: APIs deprecated in Tornado 5.1 have been removed. This includes the tornado.stack_context module

subject: callback arguments throughout tornado
truth: Stop passing callback arguments to tornado functions; use coroutine interfaces instead.
quote: most callback arguments throughout the package. All removed APIs emitted DeprecationWarning when used in Tornado 5.1

subject: WebSocketHandler.get
new: WebSocketHandler.get is now a coroutine
truth: Override WebSocketHandler.get as a coroutine (await it) in subclasses; it is now a coroutine.
quote: .WebSocketHandler.get is now a coroutine and must be called accordingly in any subclasses that override this method

subject: OAuthMixin._oauth_get_user
truth: Stop overriding _oauth_get_user; override _oauth_get_user_future instead.
quote: The OAuthMixin._oauth_get_user method has been removed. Override ~.OAuthMixin._oauth_get_user_future instead.

subject: run_on_executor callback argument
truth: Stop passing callback to run_on_executor; use the coroutine interface instead.
quote: The callback argument to .run_on_executor has been removed.

subject: return_future
truth: Stop using return_future; it has been removed.
quote: return_future has been removed.

subject: tornado.gen legacy APIs
old: engine, YieldPoint, Callback, Wait, WaitAll, MultiYieldPoint, Task
truth: Stop using engine, YieldPoint, Callback, Wait, WaitAll, MultiYieldPoint, and Task from tornado.gen; they have been removed.
quote: Some older portions of this module have been removed. This includes engine, YieldPoint, Callback, Wait, WaitAll, MultiYieldPoint, and Task.

from_fact: engine, yieldpoint, callback, wait, waitall, multiyieldpoint, task

subject: @gen.coroutine decorated functions accepting callback
old: Functions decorated with @gen.coroutine accepted callback arguments
new: Functions decorated with @gen.coroutine no longer accept callback arguments
truth: Stop passing callback arguments to functions decorated with @gen.coroutine; they are no longer accepted.
quote: Functions decorated with @gen.coroutine no longer accept callback arguments.

from_fact: functions decorated with @gen.coroutine accepted callback arguments
functions decorated with @gen.coroutine no longer accept callback arguments

subject: raise_error=False behavior
new: raise_error=False only suppresses errors from non-200 status codes
truth: raise_error=False now only suppresses errors from completed responses with non-200 status codes, not all errors.
quote: The behavior of raise_error=False has changed. Now only suppresses the errors raised due to completed responses with non-200 status codes (previously it suppressed all errors).

from_fact: raise_error=false only suppresses errors from non-200 status codes

subject: AsyncHTTPClient.fetch callback argument
truth: Stop passing callback to AsyncHTTPClient.fetch; use the coroutine interface instead.
quote: The callback argument to .AsyncHTTPClient.fetch has been removed.

subject: HTTPServerRequest.write
truth: Stop using HTTPServerRequest.write; use request.connection methods instead.
quote: HTTPServerRequest.write has been removed. Use the methods of request.connection instead.

subject: IOLoop methods
truth: Stop using IOLoop.set_blocking_signal_threshold, set_blocking_log_threshold, log_stack, and handle_callback_exception; they have been removed.
quote: IOLoop.set_blocking_signal_threshold, IOLoop.set_blocking_log_threshold, IOLoop.log_stack, and IOLoop.handle_callback_exception have been removed.

subject: streaming_callback arguments
old: streaming_callback arguments to read_bytes and read_until_close
truth: Stop passing streaming_callback to BaseIOStream.read_bytes and read_until_close; they have been removed.

subject: TornadoReactor and TwistedIOLoop
truth: Stop using TornadoReactor and TwistedIOLoop from tornado.platform.twisted; they have been removed.
quote: TornadoReactor and TwistedIOLoop have been removed.

subject: Python 3.7 support
truth: Stop using Python 3.7; the minimum supported Python version is now 3.8.
quote: Python 3.7 is no longer supported; the minimum supported Python version is 3.8.

expectlog matches warning and above regardless of current logging config (unless level argument is used)

new: Connection: close header is sent with the response
